topic: A person accidentally sends a text to the wrong recipient
raw_output:
[story 1]

The screen glowed accusingly in Clara's hand, a tiny, bright beacon of impending disaster. Her fingers hovered over the keyboard, a knot tightening in her stomach. She had meant to send a silly meme to her friend, but her thumb had slipped, landing squarely on the contact labeled 'Mr. Harrison,' a man she barely knew. A wave of hot flush crept up her neck, making her cheeks burn. *Oh no. Oh no, oh no.* Her breath hitched, shallow and quick. She watched the 'Send' button, a heavy, leaden weight pressing down on her chest. A small, involuntary tremor ran through her hand. She desperately hoped the message would somehow vanish into the ether, a digital ghost of her clumsiness.

[story 2]

Mark stared at the phone, the notification banner mocking him from the top of the screen. He hadn't intended to text that number. It was a work contact, someone intimidating, and the content of his hastily typed message—a deeply personal, rambling thought he’d been wrestling with all morning—now hung suspended between him and an unknown audience. A cold slickness coated the back of his throat. His palms started to sweat, making the phone slick against his skin. He squeezed his eyes shut, wishing the floor would open up and swallow him whole. Every nerve ending felt exposed, screaming a silent alarm. He couldn't breathe properly, just the frantic thumping in his chest echoing the panic rising in his throat.

[story 3]

Elara reread the message three times, the words blurring slightly as she looked at them. It was a confession, a raw outpouring of feelings she’d never dared voice aloud. Now, it was out there, addressed to someone who held a position of significant power in her life. A dizzying sense of exposure washed over her, leaving her feeling strangely lightheaded. Her hands trembled so violently she had to brace the phone against her thigh. She could feel the silence of her apartment amplifying the frantic rhythm of her pulse. Every muscle in her body seemed tense, coiled tight, waiting for some terrible consequence to unfold.
